sections.title: Foundational Synthesis — Fuel Classification & Strategic Importance
required_elements: Nuclear fuel cycle classification (mining & milling, conversion, enrichment, fuel fabrication)
Percentage of total cost at each stage assessment
Binding constraint identification (which stage limits new reactor deployment)
Investment vehicle landscape mapping (physical uranium, ETFs, mining equities, enrichment, utilities, futures)

sections.title: Supply/Demand Fundamentals — The Nuclear Fuel Balance Sheet
required_elements: Primary mine production tonnage (Mlbs U3O8), geographic concentration, and cost curve
Secondary supply components (government stockpile, underfeeding, MOX, commercial inventory)
Conversion and enrichment capacity utilization with planned expansions
Russian share of global SWU capacity assessment
Existing reactor fleet demand (440 reactors, ~180 Mlbs U3O8/year requirements)
New reactor construction pipeline (under construction, planned, proposed by country)
SMR and HALEU demand assessment with deployment timelines
Contract vs. spot market split (~85% contract, ~15% spot typical)
Supply/demand deficit or surplus calculation (trailing 3 years, forward 5-year)
Secondary supply drawdown trajectory
Utility inventory coverage (months of forward coverage)
Uncovered requirements ratio (percentage of forward demand not contracted)

sections.title: Macro Regime & Policy Driver Analysis
required_elements: Pro-nuclear policy shifts mapping (nuclear in clean energy taxonomy)
COP28/29 commitments and triple nuclear capacity pledge
IRA and ADVANCE Act impacts (tax credits, HALEU funding)
AI/data center demand catalyst (hyperscaler PPAs, baseload demand increases)
Russia enrichment dependency (Rosatom ~46% of global SWU, 2028 ban timeline, waivers)
Kazakhstan risk assessment (Kazatomprom 43% global mine production, geopolitical alignment)
Western supply chain rebuilding (US/EU/Canada efforts for onshore enrichment, conversion, fabrication)
Sanctions and trade policy exposure (Russian uranium ban, Section 232, TENEX status)

sections.title: Financial Logic & Valuation
required_elements: Marginal cost of production analysis (incentive price for new Tier 1 production: $65-75/lb greenfield, $50-60 ISL restart)
Replacement cost analysis (secondary supply depletion requiring primary production at higher marginal costs)
Contract vs. spot price differential analysis (Ux term vs. spot, contract premiums indicating utility urgency)
Historical real price regime analysis (2007 peak $137/lb, Fukushima trough $18/lb, current recovery)
Inventory-adjusted supply/demand model with secondary supply depletion
Positioning-based analysis (physical trust, ETF flows, speculative futures positioning)

sections.title: Pattern Matching & IDP Flagging
required_elements: Spot price rising while term contract price flat (speculative/financial buying, potentially fragile)
Utility uncovered requirements increasing with declining inventories (forced contracting cycle indicator)
Physical trust accumulation accelerating (supply tightness amplification)
Reactor announcements exceeding construction starts (pipeline inflation without execution)
Enrichment/conversion bottleneck tightening (SWU prices rising faster than U3O8)
Russia sanctions escalation (enrichment SWU supply compression by ~46%)
Mine production missing guidance (supply deficit compounding)
AI/data center nuclear PPAs increasing (10-20 year fuel demand visibility per PPA)

sections.title: Investigation Tracks
required_elements: Fuel cycle supply chain mapping (conversion capacity utilization, enrichment orderbook/SWU pricing, HALEU production, fabrication lead times)
Utility contracting cycle verification (long-term contract signings, RFP issuances, uncovered requirements projections)
Reactor pipeline reality check (WNA database vs. actual construction progress, regulatory approval, financing, grid connection dates)

structural_requirements: All sections present with markdown headings
Fuel cycle stages mapped with cost breakdown (% mining, conversion, enrichment, fabrication)
Binding constraint identified (which stage limits deployment)
Investment vehicle types documented with exposure profiles
Mine production tonnage documented with geographic concentration (Kazakhstan ~43%, Canada ~15%, Namibia ~11%, Australia ~10%)
Cost curve distribution documented (cash cost percentiles)
Secondary supply components quantified with depletion timeline
Enrichment capacity and Russian SWU share documented (~46% Rosatom control)
Existing reactor fleet fuel demand calculated (~180 Mlbs U3O8/year)
New reactor pipeline quantified (under construction, planned, proposed) with country breakdown
SMR deployment timeline and fuel type requirements documented (LEU vs. HALEU)
HALEU demand assessed with only Centrus Energy currently licensed for US production
Contract vs. spot market split documented (~85% term, ~15% spot)
Supply/demand balance calculated (trailing 3 years, forward 5-year with secondary supply)
Utility inventory coverage (months) documented with decline trends
Uncovered requirements ratio calculated (leading contracting indicator)
Pro-nuclear policy shifts documented by country
COP28/29 commitments and nuclear capacity targets listed
IRA and ADVANCE Act tax credits and HALEU funding quantified
AI/data center PPAs tracked (MW contracted, fuel demand implications)
Russia enrichment sanctions timeline and impact (2028 ban, waivers, workarounds)
Kazakhstan Kazatomprom risk assessed (state ownership, geopolitical alignment, production guidance)
Incentive price for new Tier 1 production identified
Contract vs. spot price differential documented (Ux term premium magnitude)
Real price vs. historical peak/trough calculated
Investigation tracks include specific fuel cycle entities (ConverDyn, Orano, Centrus, Rosatom, URENCO), RFP tracking, and WNA reactor database verification
